title: Add default worktree isolation and completion PR creation to hrn
goal: Add an hrn CLI --worktree option defaulting to true, create and track a dedicated .hrn worktree and branch for each workflow, reject edits outside the active worktree, and mechanically create a PR when all workflow work is complete as part of the complete transition.

acceptanceCriteria.description: hrn accepts --worktree true|false, defaults to true, and uses a .hrn worktree only in enabled mode; false preserves the current-directory mode without worktree checks or forced PR creation.

Enabled mode creates/reuses a main-based, uniquely named hrn/* branch and .hrn worktree through a validated external mapping, updates the branch from the first workflow request, and safely creates anew for absent, stale, or malformed mappings.

Harness knows the active worktree, rejects direct outside-worktree file mutations and external-worktree Git targeting, and rejects commits while .hrn is not ignored; agent guidance instructs the agent to add the ignore rule before committing.

After all current workflow units and verification succeed, enabled mode obtains PR metadata from the agent, pushes the mapped branch to origin, creates a main-targeting gh PR with the supplied metadata, retries failures at most three times, preserves evidence, reports terminal failure, and still completes; disabled mode skips this contract.
Focused automated coverage exercises CLI parsing, map lifecycle, isolation/commit guards, and successful and exhausted completion-PR paths; the maintained repository test suite passes.

workUnits.title: Implement CLI worktree mode and mapped workspace lifecycle
workUnits.purpose: Create the reusable runtime component that owns enabled/disabled mode parsing, .hrn workspace mapping, safe reuse/recreation, main-based random branches, and post-intake branch naming without placing workspace data in workflow artifacts.
verification: Add focused automated assertions for boolean option parsing, disabled-mode bypass, initial creation from main, valid mapping reuse, and stale/malformed mapping replacement.
Inspect that workspace path and branch are stored only in the .hrn mapping rather than .engineering-harness workflow artifacts.
stopConditions: Stop and reopen refinement if Git cannot provide a local main base or if a required resume-selection interface is ambiguous.
Do not alter workflow artifact schemas to persist worktree metadata.

workUnits.title: Enforce workspace boundaries and completion PR transition
workUnits.purpose: Connect the mapped active workspace to Guardian authorization, branch attachment/renaming at workflow intake, .hrn ignore commit checks, agent guidance, and the final retrying origin/gh PR transition.
verification: Add focused automated Guardian tests for direct external file/Git rejection, current-worktree allowance, and blocked commits until .hrn is ignored.

Use controlled Git/GitHub CLI fixtures to prove PR metadata is requested and supplied metadata drives push/create retries, evidence persistence, terminal failure reporting, and completion; prove disabled mode bypasses the PR transition.
stopConditions: Do not reject reads, symlink traversal, cwd changes, or lexical path traversal solely because they can have benign uses.
Do not make successful PR creation a permanent completion blocker after the third failed attempt.

workUnits.title: Complete regression coverage and independent verification
workUnits.purpose: Integrate the new lifecycle coverage into the maintained shell acceptance suite and independently validate the final runtime behavior and packaging contract.
verification: Run npm ci --ignore-scripts and npm test in the final worktree.
Independently review the final diff for unintended paths, .hrn packaging/ignore leaks, lifecycle coverage gaps, and actual PR-failure evidence handling.
stopConditions: Stop on any failing maintained check; report the failing command and do not claim a passing receipt.
Do not modify release versions, publish configuration, or unrelated installer behavior.
